label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's figure out which of the two statements is sarcastic. I need to understand the options first. Option A: Good thing you can boil impurities out of water. That sounds like a genuine statement. Boiling water is a common method to remove impurities, like bacteria, which is a standard practice in many places. So the person is probably expressing gratitude for that method. Option B: Good thing you can boil Uranium out of water. Hmm, Uranium is a heavy metal, a radioactive element. If water is contaminated with Uranium, boiling it won't remove it. In fact, boiling might even concentrate the Uranium if any water evaporates. So this statement seems factually incorrect. If someone says "Good thing..." but the method they're referring to is actually not effective, that's probably sarcasm. So the sarcasm in option B comes from the speaker pretending to be grateful for a method that doesn't actually work. They might be commenting on someone else's
